prompt: Translate this English proverb into two other languages in a way that preserves the spirit, not the words: 'The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.' Add a one-sentence note on each.
response:
Spanish  
«El mejor momento para cultivar un olivo fue hace veinte años; el siguiente mejor momento es hoy mismo.»  
Note: In Spain, the olive is an age-old symbol of perseverance and nourishment, keeping the proverb rooted (literally) in shared cultural soil.  

Japanese  
「永久（とわ）に実を結ばせるなら二十年前、次善はこの瞬間。」  
Note: Replacing the literal tree with the idea of something that “bears fruit eternally” fits the Japanese fondness for poetic brevity and forward-looking determination.
